question: Describe the main limitations of file-based data management systems that led to the development of Database Management Systems (DBMS).
expectedPoints: Difficulty in accessing data and lack of data integration
Concurrent access anomalies and security issues

question: Explain the significance of the relational model, proposed by E.F. Codd, in the history of database applications.
expectedPoints: Introduced data independence by separating the logical and physical views of data
Provided a simple, mathematical foundation for data modeling using relations (tables)
Enabled the development of high-level query languages (e.g., SQL) for data access and manipulation
Became the dominant model, forming the basis for most modern DBMS

question: Identify two early database models that preceded the relational model.
